item.title: Tunable Chern insulator states with coexisting magnonic and electronic topology in two-dimensional honeycomb Kitaev ferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Haozhou Cai, Zhiming Xu, Jian Wu, and Weiyi Pan

The coexistence of topological magnons and electrons in magnetic materials presents a compelling route toward developing low-dissipation, multifunctional spintronic devices. However, material systems enabling their simultaneous realization and control remain largely unexplored. Here, we propose the …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134413] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haozhou Cai, Zhiming Xu, Jian Wu, and Weiyi Pan

The coexistence of topological magnons and electrons in magnetic materials presents a compelling route toward developing low-dissipation, multifunctional spintronic devices. However, material systems enabling their simultaneous realization and control remain largely unexplored. Here, we propose the …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134413] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.dc:title: Tunable Chern insulator states with coexisting magnonic and electronic topology in two-dimensional honeycomb Kitaev ferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Haozhou Cai, Zhiming Xu, Jian Wu, and Weiyi Pan

item.title: Tunneling in multisite mesoscopic quantum Hall circuits
item.description:
Author(s): D. B. Karki

Transport properties of single- and two-site mesoscopic quantum Hall (QH) circuits at high transparencies can be described in terms of the lowest-order backscattering processes, enabling a mapping to the boundary sine-Gordon model. We show that this description breaks down in circuits with four or m…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155411] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): D. B. Karki

Transport properties of single- and two-site mesoscopic quantum Hall (QH) circuits at high transparencies can be described in terms of the lowest-order backscattering processes, enabling a mapping to the boundary sine-Gordon model. We show that this description breaks down in circuits with four or m…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155411] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.dc:title: Tunneling in multisite mesoscopic quantum Hall circuits
item.dc:creator: D. B. Karki
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Absence of parity anomaly in massive Dirac fermions on a lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Shun-Qing Shen

The author shows here that the parity anomaly or half quantized Hall effect is absent in two-dimensional massive Dirac fermions and, instead, is an intrinsic property of a single massless Dirac cone on a lattice. The result subverts the longstanding theory of parity anomaly in massive Dirac fermions and challenges its extensive applications in condensed matter physics, such as the quantum valley Hall effect in 2D materials and metamaterials, and the half-quantized surface Hall effect and related effects in topological insulators.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155412] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shun-Qing Shen

The author shows here that the parity anomaly or half quantized Hall effect is absent in two-dimensional massive Dirac fermions and, instead, is an intrinsic property of a single massless Dirac cone on a lattice. The result subverts the longstanding theory of parity anomaly in massive Dirac fermions and challenges its extensive applications in condensed matter physics, such as the quantum valley Hall effect in 2D materials and metamaterials, and the half-quantized surface Hall effect and related effects in topological insulators.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155412] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.dc:title: Absence of parity anomaly in massive Dirac fermions on a lattice
item.dc:creator: Shun-Qing Shen

item.title: Topological electronic states, superconductivity, and ferromagnetism in two-dimensional $\mathrm{Sc}X{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$ ($X$=Rh, Ta, Mo) monolayers
item.description:
Author(s): Haifei Qin and Hong Sun

The two-dimensional (2D) materials with coexistence of Dirac points and Van Hove singularities (VHSs) provide a promising platform for seeking topological superconductivity or ferromagnetism at high critical temperatures. However, ideal examples combining electronic topological properties with super…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155413] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

The two-dimensional (2D) materials with coexistence of Dirac points and Van Hove singularities (VHSs) provide a promising platform for seeking topological superconductivity or ferromagnetism at high critical temperatures. However, ideal examples combining electronic topological properties with super…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155413] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

item.title: Nonlinearity-driven topology via spontaneous symmetry breaking
item.description:
Author(s): Alessandro Coppo, Alexandre Le Boité, Simone Felicetti, and Valentina Brosco

Topology and nonlinearity are deeply connected. However, whether topological effects can arise solely from the structure of nonlinear interaction terms, and the nature of the resulting topological phases, remain to large extent open questions. Here we consider a chain of parametrically driven quantu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155415] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alessandro Coppo, Alexandre Le Boité, Simone Felicetti, and Valentina Brosco

Topology and nonlinearity are deeply connected. However, whether topological effects can arise solely from the structure of nonlinear interaction terms, and the nature of the resulting topological phases, remain to large extent open questions. Here we consider a chain of parametrically driven quantu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155415] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

item.title: Specific heat and density anomaly in the Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): M. A. Habitzreuter, Willdauany C. de Freitas Silva, Eduardo O. Rizzatti, Thereza Paiva, and Marcia C. Barbosa

Understanding thermal properties of materials is fundamental to technological applications and to discovering new phenomena. In particular, advances in experimental techniques such as cold-atom measurements allow the simulation of paradigmatic Hamiltonians with great control over model parameters, s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165111] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

In particular, advances in experimental techniques such as cold-atom measurements allow the simulation of paradigmatic Hamiltonians with great control over model parameters, s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165111] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

Author(s): Kaila Jenkins, Yuan Zhu, Dechen Zhang, Guoxin Zheng, Kuan-Wen Chen, Aaron Chan, Sijie Xu, Mason L. Klemm, Bin Gao, Ming Yi, Pengcheng Dai, and Lu Li

Kagome metals provide a material platform for probing new correlated quantum phenomena due to the naturally incorporated linear dispersions, flat bands, and Van Hove singularities in their electronic structures. Among these quantum phenomena is the charge density wave (CDW), or the distortion of the…

Kagome metals provide a material platform for probing new correlated quantum phenomena due to the naturally incorporated linear dispersions, flat bands, and Van Hove singularities in their electronic structures. Among these quantum phenomena is the charge density wave (CDW), or the distortion of the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165112] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

item.title: Topological thermal transport of sliding electron crystals
item.description:
Author(s): Ning-Jing Yang, Zhigao Huang, and Jian-Min Zhang

Topological thermal transport offers a quantum route to regulate heat flow through Berry-curvature-driven responses. However, approaches for dynamically switchable control of such transport are lacking. Here, we propose an interlayer-sliding-driven dynamical evolution mechanism to control topologica…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165408] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ning-Jing Yang, Zhigao Huang, and Jian-Min Zhang

Topological thermal transport offers a quantum route to regulate heat flow through Berry-curvature-driven responses. However, approaches for dynamically switchable control of such transport are lacking. Here, we propose an interlayer-sliding-driven dynamical evolution mechanism to control topologica…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165408] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026

item.title: Gate tuning of multichannel electronic transport in three-dimensional ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{y}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{2y}{\mathrm{Te}}_{3x}{\mathrm{Se}}_{x}$ topological insulator films
item.description:
Author(s): N. P. Stepina, A. O. Bazhenov, A. V. Shumilin, J. J. Baldoví, A. V. Nenashev, E. Yu. Zhdanov, D. V. Ishchenko, M. S. Aksenov, and O. E. Tereshchenko

We report a gate-tunable magnetoresistance and nonlinear Hall effect in thin ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{y}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{2−y}{\mathrm{Te}}_{3−x}{\mathrm{Se}}_{x}$ films grown by molecular beam epitaxy. The Hall coefficient ${R}_{H}$ exhibits distinct nonlinear behavior controlled by the gate voltage: a $∼2%−3%…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165409] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): N. P. Stepina, A. O. Bazhenov, A. V. Shumilin, J. J. Baldoví, A. V. Nenashev, E. Yu. Zhdanov, D. V. Ishchenko, M. S. Aksenov, and O. E. Tereshchenko

We report a gate-tunable magnetoresistance and nonlinear Hall effect in thin BiySb2−yTe3−xSex films grown by molecular beam epitaxy. The Hall coefficient RH exhibits distinct nonlinear behavior controlled by the gate voltage: a ∼2%−3% increase at low field (B∼0.5T), opposite slopes for positive and …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165409] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.dc:title: Gate tuning of multichannel electronic transport in three-dimensional ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{y}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{2y}{\mathrm{Te}}_{3x}{\mathrm{Se}}_{x}$ topological insulator films

item.title: Topological point states in non-Hermitian quasicrystals
item.description:
Author(s): Jianzhi Chen, Aoqian Shi, Yuchen Peng, Peng Peng, and Jianjun Liu

Topological point states (TPSs) have the same physical mechanism as that of the topological corner states (TCSs) in higher-order topological insulators (HOTIs) in two-dimensional (2D) systems and bring a new physical perspective and application prospect to HOTI. However, the current research on TPSs…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134106] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026

However, the current research on TPSs…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134106] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026

item.title: Pairing around a single Dirac point: A unifying view of Kohn-Luttinger superconductivity in Chern bands, quarter metals, and topological surface states
item.description:
Author(s): Omid Tavakol and Thomas Scaffidi

Superconductivity of a single two-dimensional Dirac fermion offers a natural route to topological superconductivity. While usually considered extrinsic—arising from proximity to a conventional superconductor—we investigate when a doped Dirac cone can spontaneously develop superconductivity from a sh…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144502] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Omid Tavakol and Thomas Scaffidi

Superconductivity of a single two-dimensional Dirac fermion offers a natural route to topological superconductivity. While usually considered extrinsic—arising from proximity to a conventional superconductor—we investigate when a doped Dirac cone can spontaneously develop superconductivity from a sh…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144502] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.dc:title: Pairing around a single Dirac point: A unifying view of Kohn-Luttinger superconductivity in Chern bands, quarter metals, and topological surface states
item.dc:creator: Omid Tavakol and Thomas Scaffidi
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Spin-orbit driven topological phases in kagome materials
item.description:
Author(s): Chi Wu and Tiantian Zhang

Here, the authors investigate spin-orbit coupling (SOC) effects in kagome-type IAMX materials through a combination of theoretical modeling and first-principles calculations (IA = alkali metal, M = rare earth metal, and X = carbon group element). By developing a minimal four-band spinful model, the study captures SOC-induced topological phase transitions, illustrating the continuous evolution of phase diagrams and topological surface states. The model is supported by systematic DFT calculations across different materials, bridging theoretical models with real-world materials. This research serves as a valuable guide for leveraging IAMX materials in multifunctional device applications.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155115] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chi Wu and Tiantian Zhang

Here, the authors investigate spin-orbit coupling (SOC) effects in kagome-type IAMX materials through a combination of theoretical modeling and first-principles calculations (IA = alkali metal, M = rare earth metal, and X = carbon group element). By developing a minimal four-band spinful model, the study captures SOC-induced topological phase transitions, illustrating the continuous evolution of phase diagrams and topological surface states. The model is supported by systematic DFT calculations across different materials, bridging theoretical models with real-world materials. This research serves as a valuable guide for leveraging IAMX materials in multifunctional device applications.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155115] Published Tue Apr 07, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-orbit driven topological phases in kagome materials
item.dc:creator: Chi Wu and Tiantian Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Revised crystal structure, disordered spin dynamics, and dichotomous magnetic excitations in a field-induced intermediate state of the honeycomb Kitaev magnet ${\mathrm{Na}}_{2}{\mathrm{Co}}_{2}{\mathrm{TeO}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): Suheon Lee, Poonam Yadav, Raju Kalaivanan, Xianghan Xu, Kapil Kumar, Matthias J. Gutmann, Christian Balz, J. Ross Stewart, Chennan Wang, Zurab Guguchia, Hubertus Luetkens, Sang-Wook Cheong, Raman Sankar, Kwang-Yong Choi, and Sungkyun Choi

Here, the authors report the dichotomous nature of magnetic excitations in a field-induced intermediate state of Na2Co2TeO6, a leading candidate for a Kitaev quantum spin liquid in 3d-based systems. Using inelastic neutron scattering and muon experiments, they observe low-energy magnons and high-energy spinons in high magnetic fields, along with disordered spin dynamics. A revised crystal structure reveals a triangular Na layer that can better stabilize the spin liquid state under magnetic fields.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134411] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Suheon Lee, Poonam Yadav, Raju Kalaivanan, Xianghan Xu, Kapil Kumar, Matthias J. Gutmann, Christian Balz, J. Ross Stewart, Chennan Wang, Zurab Guguchia, Hubertus Luetkens, Sang-Wook Cheong, Raman Sankar, Kwang-Yong Choi, and Sungkyun Choi

Here, the authors report the dichotomous nature of magnetic excitations in a field-induced intermediate state of Na2Co2TeO6, a leading candidate for a Kitaev quantum spin liquid in 3d-based systems. Using inelastic neutron scattering and muon experiments, they observe low-energy magnons and high-energy spinons in high magnetic fields, along with disordered spin dynamics. A revised crystal structure reveals a triangular Na layer that can better stabilize the spin liquid state under magnetic fields.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134411] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Revised crystal structure, disordered spin dynamics, and dichotomous magnetic excitations in a field-induced intermediate state of the honeycomb Kitaev magnet ${\mathrm{Na}}_{2}{\mathrm{Co}}_{2}{\mathrm{TeO}}_{6}$
item.dc:creator: Suheon Lee, Poonam Yadav, Raju Kalaivanan, Xianghan Xu, Kapil Kumar, Matthias J. Gutmann, Christian Balz, J. Ross Stewart, Chennan Wang, Zurab Guguchia, Hubertus Luetkens, Sang-Wook Cheong, Raman Sankar, Kwang-Yong Choi, and Sungkyun Choi

item.title: Holon metal, charge density wave metal, and chiral superconductor from doping a fractional Chern insulator and an ${\mathrm{SU}(3)}_{1}$ chiral spin liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Ya-Hui Zhang

Recent experiments have observed superconductivity proximate to the $ν=−2/3$ fractional quantum anomalous Hall (FQAH) insulator in twisted ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$. A critical open question is whether the underlying normal state is a Fermi liquid with a large Fermi surface or a strongly correlated meta…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ya-Hui Zhang

Recent experiments have observed superconductivity proximate to the ν=−2/3 fractional quantum anomalous Hall (FQAH) insulator in twisted MoTe2. A critical open question is whether the underlying normal state is a Fermi liquid with a large Fermi surface or a strongly correlated metal with low carrier…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026

item.title: Dynamical correlations and Mott localization in a paramagnetic ${\mathrm{VI}}_{3}$ bulk crystal
item.description:
Author(s): Luis Craco and Sabrina S. Carara

We perform a comprehensive study on the correlated electronic structure reconstruction of paramagnetic ${\mathrm{VI}}_{3}$ crystal in its rhombohedral and monoclinic structural phases. Using density-functional plus dynamical mean-field theory ($\mathrm{DFT}+\mathrm{DMFT}$) calculations, we explicitl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155113] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Luis Craco and Sabrina S. Carara

We perform a comprehensive study on the correlated electronic structure reconstruction of paramagnetic VI3 crystal in its rhombohedral and monoclinic structural phases. Using density-functional plus dynamical mean-field theory (DFT+DMFT) calculations, we explicitly demonstrate the importance of loca…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155113] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026

item.title: Electron energy loss function in a graphene-hBN-graphene nanostructure
item.description:
Author(s): Ana Kalinić, Vito Despoja, Ivan Radović, Lazar Karbunar, and Zoran L. Mišković

The aim of this study is to theoretically investigate the loss function of ${\mathrm{layer}}_{1}$-hBN-${\mathrm{layer}}_{2}$ nanostructures, where layers 1 and 2 may be doped graphene, pristine graphene, or vacuum. This was achieved by comparing results obtained from the ab initio and massless Dirac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155408] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ana Kalinić, Vito Despoja, Ivan Radović, Lazar Karbunar, and Zoran L. Mišković

The aim of this study is to theoretically investigate the loss function of layer1-hBN-layer2 nanostructures, where layers 1 and 2 may be doped graphene, pristine graphene, or vacuum. This was achieved by comparing results obtained from the ab initio and massless Dirac fermion approaches and by exami…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155408] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026

item.title: Non-Abelian topological edge states in a one-dimensional ladder system with flat bands
item.description:
Author(s): Xu-Jin Wang, Yu-Peng Li, Zhigang Wang, and Jie-Yun Yan

Non-Abelian topological theory enables the classification of multigap systems and captures complex structures beyond conventional band topology. In this study, we propose a four-band non-Abelian topological insulator model in the one-dimensional ladder lattice. This model features a pair of flat ban…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155409] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xu-Jin Wang, Yu-Peng Li, Zhigang Wang, and Jie-Yun Yan

Non-Abelian topological theory enables the classification of multigap systems and captures complex structures beyond conventional band topology. In this study, we propose a four-band non-Abelian topological insulator model in the one-dimensional ladder lattice. This model features a pair of flat ban…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155409] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Partition function of the Kitaev quantum double model
item.description:
Author(s): Anna Ritz-Zwilling, Benoît Douçot, Steven H. Simon, Julien Vidal, and Jean-Noël Fuchs

Thirty years ago, Kitaev first brought together the ideas of quantum information and topological order in a landmark paper, introducing the famous toric code and its generalization, the so-called quantum double model. In some cases, this model displays non-Abelian anyons that can, in principle, perform universal topological quantum computation. Here, the authors present the first complete solution of the quantum double model (and some of its extensions), including the full eigenspectrum and its exact finite-temperature partition function.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165106] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Anna Ritz-Zwilling, Benoît Douçot, Steven H. Simon, Julien Vidal, and Jean-Noël Fuchs

Thirty years ago, Kitaev first brought together the ideas of quantum information and topological order in a landmark paper, introducing the famous toric code and its generalization, the so-called quantum double model. In some cases, this model displays non-Abelian anyons that can, in principle, perform universal topological quantum computation. Here, the authors present the first complete solution of the quantum double model (and some of its extensions), including the full eigenspectrum and its exact finite-temperature partition function.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165106] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Partition function of the Kitaev quantum double model
item.dc:creator: Anna Ritz-Zwilling, Benoît Douçot, Steven H. Simon, Julien Vidal, and Jean-Noël Fuchs
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Efficient prediction of topological superlattice bands with spin-orbit coupling
item.description:
Author(s): M. Nabil Y. Lhachemi, Valentin Crépel, and Jennifer Cano

The authors introduce here a symmetry‑indicator approach that predicts the topology of superlattice‑induced minibands in the presence of spin‑orbit coupling. By determining how superlattice harmonics determine symmetry eigenvalues at the high‑symmetry points of the folded Brillouin zone, the method yields ℤ2 indices and Chern numbers without full band‑structure calculations. The analysis reveals clear conditions under which patterned superlattices generate topological minibands — even when the parent system is topologically trivial. The criterion is applied across diverse material platforms, including thin films of topological insulators and transition metal dichalcogenides.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165109] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. Nabil Y. Lhachemi, Valentin Crépel, and Jennifer Cano

The authors introduce here a symmetry‑indicator approach that predicts the topology of superlattice‑induced minibands in the presence of spin‑orbit coupling. By determining how superlattice harmonics determine symmetry eigenvalues at the high‑symmetry points of the folded Brillouin zone, the method yields ℤ2 indices and Chern numbers without full band‑structure calculations. The analysis reveals clear conditions under which patterned superlattices generate topological minibands — even when the parent system is topologically trivial. The criterion is applied across diverse material platforms, including thin films of topological insulators and transition metal dichalcogenides.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165109] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Efficient prediction of topological superlattice bands with spin-orbit coupling
item.dc:creator: M. Nabil Y. Lhachemi, Valentin Crépel, and Jennifer Cano
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Interplay between non-Fermi liquid and non-Hermiticity: A multimethod study of non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo model
item.description:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

The authors construct here an open system setup that realizes a non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo problem. By applying multiple nonperturbative analytic and numerical methods, they identify three phases enriched by non-Hermiticity. The computed impurity entropy exceeds the free local moment value of ln2, indicating the emergence of a Yu–Shiba–Rusinov-like impurity state. The corresponding Kondo conductance exhibits an anomalous temperature scaling induced by non-Hermiticity, pointing to transport phenomena distinct from those in conventional Hermitian Kondo systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

The authors construct here an open system setup that realizes a non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo problem. By applying multiple nonperturbative analytic and numerical methods, they identify three phases enriched by non-Hermiticity. The computed impurity entropy exceeds the free local moment value of ln2, indicating the emergence of a Yu–Shiba–Rusinov-like impurity state. The corresponding Kondo conductance exhibits an anomalous temperature scaling induced by non-Hermiticity, pointing to transport phenomena distinct from those in conventional Hermitian Kondo systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165110] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Interplay between non-Fermi liquid and non-Hermiticity: A multimethod study of non-Hermitian multichannel Kondo model
item.dc:creator: Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Strain- and doping-tunable optical resonance in Kekulé-Y graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Yawar Mohammadi

We investigate the optical response of Kekulé-Y graphene under uniaxial strain and carrier doping. Using a low-energy effective Hamiltonian, we show that strain reshapes the low-energy electronic structure of the Kekulé-Y phase and induces van Hove singularities at energies well below those of prist…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165406] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yawar Mohammadi

We investigate the optical response of Kekulé-Y graphene under uniaxial strain and carrier doping. Using a low-energy effective Hamiltonian, we show that strain reshapes the low-energy electronic structure of the Kekulé-Y phase and induces van Hove singularities at energies well below those of prist…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165406] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Strain- and doping-tunable optical resonance in Kekulé-Y graphene
item.dc:creator: Yawar Mohammadi
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Observation of Floquet topological corner and hinge states via higher-order topolectrical space-time circuits
item.description:
Author(s): Long Qian, Weixuan Zhang, Wenhui Cao, Fengxiao Di, Xiaoqi Zhou, and Xiangdong Zhang

Floquet higher-order topological insulators (HOTIs), hosting robust corner-/hinge-localized states under periodic driving, represent a pivotal frontier in nonequilibrium topological physics. Although theoretical studies suggest that nonsymmorphic space-time symmetries could stabilize exotic Floquet …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165407] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Long Qian, Weixuan Zhang, Wenhui Cao, Fengxiao Di, Xiaoqi Zhou, and Xiangdong Zhang

Floquet higher-order topological insulators (HOTIs), hosting robust corner-/hinge-localized states under periodic driving, represent a pivotal frontier in nonequilibrium topological physics. Although theoretical studies suggest that nonsymmorphic space-time symmetries could stabilize exotic Floquet …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165407] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026

item.title: Quantum phase diagram of the spin-$\frac{1}{2}$ Heisenberg antiferromagnet on the square-kagome lattice: A tensor network study
item.description:
Author(s): Saeed S. Jahromi and Yasir Iqbal

We study the ground-state phase diagram of the spin-$1/2$ antiferromagnetic Heisenberg model on the square-kagome lattice using infinite projected entangled-pair states. By systematically varying the ratio of exchange couplings on triangular and square plaquettes, we establish a complete quantum pha…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140402] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Saeed S. Jahromi and Yasir Iqbal

We study the ground-state phase diagram of the spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic Heisenberg model on the square-kagome lattice using infinite projected entangled-pair states. By systematically varying the ratio of exchange couplings on triangular and square plaquettes, we establish a complete quantum phase…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140402] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026

item.title: Antitopological magnetic textures in an antiferromagnetically coupled bilayer with frustration
item.description:
Author(s): Lewei Zhou, Jun Chen, Zhong Shen, Shuai Dong, and Xiaoyan Yao

The bilayer skyrmion composed of upper and lower tightly coupled skyrmions on two layers with completely compensated topological charges (called as antitopology here), has become one feasible improvement of conventional skyrmion to realize straight motion without skyrmion Hall effect, which has arou…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140403] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026

The bilayer skyrmion composed of upper and lower tightly coupled skyrmions on two layers with completely compensated topological charges (called as antitopology here), has become one feasible improvement of conventional skyrmion to realize straight motion without skyrmion Hall effect, which has arou…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140403] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026

item.title: High-temperature quantum anomalous Hall effect in buckled honeycomb antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Mohsen Hafez-Torbati and Götz S. Uhrig

We propose Néel antiferromagnetic (AF) Mott insulators with a buckled honeycomb structure as potential candidates to host a high-temperature AF Chern insulator (AFCI). Using a generalized Kondo lattice model, we show that the staggered potential induced by a perpendicular electric field due to the b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161105] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mohsen Hafez-Torbati and Götz S. Uhrig

We propose Néel antiferromagnetic (AF) Mott insulators with a buckled honeycomb structure as potential candidates to host a high-temperature AF Chern insulator (AFCI). Using a generalized Kondo lattice model, we show that the staggered potential induced by a perpendicular electric field due to the b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161105] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: High-temperature quantum anomalous Hall effect in buckled honeycomb antiferromagnets

item.title: Quantum melting a Wigner crystal into Hall liquids
item.description:
Author(s): Aidan P. Reddy and Liang Fu

Recent experiments have shown that, counterintuitively, applying a magnetic field to a Wigner crystal can induce quantum Hall effects. In this Letter, we use variational Monte Carlo to show that a magnetic field can melt zero-field Wigner crystals into integer quantum Hall liquids. This melting orig…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161403] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Aidan P. Reddy and Liang Fu

Recent experiments have shown that, counterintuitively, applying a magnetic field to a Wigner crystal can induce quantum Hall effects. In this Letter, we use variational Monte Carlo to show that a magnetic field can melt zero-field Wigner crystals into integer quantum Hall liquids. This melting orig…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161403] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum melting a Wigner crystal into Hall liquids

The CoSbTe single crystal has been investigated using ab initio first-principles calculations, magnetic, heat capacity, magnetotransport, neutron diffraction, and Mössbauer measurements. Density functional theory calculations reveal band inversion with nodal-line semimetal-like crossings near the Fe…

The CoSbTe single crystal has been investigated using ab initio first-principles calculations, magnetic, heat capacity, magnetotransport, neutron diffraction, and Mössbauer measurements. Density functional theory calculations reveal band inversion with nodal-line semimetal-like crossings near the Fe…

item.description:
Author(s): Run Long, Min-Quan Kuang, Fang Yun, Fang-guang Kuang, Lai Wei, and Mingmin Zhong

Surface-functionalized two-dimensional (2D) materials have garnered considerable interest owing to their emergent functional properties, including catalytic activity, ferroelectricity, and superconductivity. Here, based on first-principle calculations, we demonstrate that selenium functionalization …

Here, based on first-principle calculations, we demonstrate that selenium functionalization …

We investigate the strain response of underdoped ${\mathrm{HgBa}}_{2}{\mathrm{CuO}}_{4+δ}$ (Hg1201), by synchrotron X-ray diffraction and corresponding simulations of thermal diffuse scattering. The compression in the crystallographic $a$ direction leads to relatively small expansion in the $b$ and …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134507] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mai Ye, Wenshan Hong, Tom Laurin Lacmann, Mehdi Frachet, Igor Vinograd, Gaston Garbarino, Sofia-Michaela Souliou, Michael Merz, Rolf Heid, Amir-Abbas Haghighirad, Yuan Li, and Matthieu Le Tacon

We investigate the strain response of underdoped HgBa2CuO4+δ (Hg1201), by synchrotron X-ray diffraction and corresponding simulations of thermal diffuse scattering. The compression in the crystallographic a direction leads to relatively small expansion in the b and c directions, with Poisson ratios …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134507] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026

item.title: Identifying the non-Hermitian skin effect in many-body dynamics by graphs
item.description:
Author(s): Peng He and Sen Lin

The non-Hermitian skin effect (NHSE) has prominently emerged in non-Hermitian systems, leading to a plethora of unique features without Hermitian counterparts. However, the characterization of NHSE in many-body dynamics remains elusive. Here we present an algebraic framework to comprehensively chara…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144302] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Peng He and Sen Lin

The non-Hermitian skin effect (NHSE) has prominently emerged in non-Hermitian systems, leading to a plethora of unique features without Hermitian counterparts. However, the characterization of NHSE in many-body dynamics remains elusive. Here we present an algebraic framework to comprehensively chara…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144302] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026

item.description:
Author(s): Xiao-Long Lü, Pei-Hao Fu, Lie-Run Tian, Xiang-Long Yu, and Jun-Feng Liu

Generating spin-mixed edge states with hybridized spin-up and spin-down components and clarifying their transport mechanisms are critical to topological spintronic and valleytronic devices. In this work, we propose two types of spatially modulated Haldane-Rashba models that incorporate spatial varia…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155403] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiao-Long Lü, Pei-Hao Fu, Lie-Run Tian, Xiang-Long Yu, and Jun-Feng Liu

Generating spin-mixed edge states with hybridized spin-up and spin-down components and clarifying their transport mechanisms are critical to topological spintronic and valleytronic devices. In this work, we propose two types of spatially modulated Haldane-Rashba models that incorporate spatial varia…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155403] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026

item.title: Kagome bands and magnetism in $\mathrm{MoT}{\mathrm{e}}_{2−x}$ kagome monolayers
item.description:
Author(s): Jiaqi Dai, Zhongqin Zhang, Zemin Pan, Cong Wang, Chendong Zhang, Zhihai Cheng, and Wei Ji

Kagome lattices facilitate various quantum phases, yet in bulk materials, their kagome flat bands often interact with bulk bands, suppressing kagome electronic characteristics for hosting these phases. Here, we use density functional theory calculations to predict the geometric and electronic struct…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155405] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiaqi Dai, Zhongqin Zhang, Zemin Pan, Cong Wang, Chendong Zhang, Zhihai Cheng, and Wei Ji

Kagome lattices facilitate various quantum phases, yet in bulk materials, their kagome flat bands often interact with bulk bands, suppressing kagome electronic characteristics for hosting these phases. Here, we use density functional theory calculations to predict the geometric and electronic struct…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155405] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026

item.title: Fluctuation response of a minimal Kitaev chain in nonequilibrium states
item.description:
Author(s): Sergey Smirnov

Minimal Kitaev chains provide a unique platform to engineer Majorana states in quantum dots interacting via normal tunneling and crossed Andreev reflection specified by their amplitudes $|{η}_{n,a}|$. Here we analyze fluctuations of electric currents in a double quantum dot Kitaev chain using the di…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165404] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sergey Smirnov

Minimal Kitaev chains provide a unique platform to engineer Majorana states in quantum dots interacting via normal tunneling and crossed Andreev reflection specified by their amplitudes |ηn,a|. Here we analyze fluctuations of electric currents in a double quantum dot Kitaev chain using the different…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165404] Published Fri Apr 03, 2026

item.description:

Materials combining electrically switchable ferroelectricity and tunable topological states hold significant promise for advancing both fundamental quantum phenomena and practical device architectures. Here, we employ first-principles calculations to systematically investigate the sliding ferroelect…

Here, we employ first-principles calculations to systematically investigate the sliding ferroelect…

item.title: Gapless symmetry-protected topological states in measurement-only circuits
item.description:
Author(s): Xue-Jia Yu, Sheng Yang, Shuo Liu, Hai-Qing Lin, and Shao-Kai Jian

Measurement-only quantum circuits offer a versatile platform for realizing intriguing quantum phases of matter. However, gapless symmetry-protected topological (gSPT) states remain insufficiently explored in these settings. Here, we generalize the notion of gSPT to the critical steady state in measu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134302] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xue-Jia Yu, Sheng Yang, Shuo Liu, Hai-Qing Lin, and Shao-Kai Jian

Measurement-only quantum circuits offer a versatile platform for realizing intriguing quantum phases of matter. However, gapless symmetry-protected topological (gSPT) states remain insufficiently explored in these settings. Here, we generalize the notion of gSPT to the critical steady state in measu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134302] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026

item.description:
Author(s): Jia Xu, Xianguo Jiang, Shaohua Zhang, Yaxuan Jin, Lei Hao, Ning Yang, Yizheng Wu, Feng Xu, Wendeng Huang, Hao Meng, Johan Åkerman, Yan Zhou, and Chao Zhou

Antiferromagnetic oxide materials are emerging as key ingredients in spintronic devices and ultrafast terahertz (THz) emitters due to their distinct spin dynamics and vanishing net magnetization. Here, we report an irreversible, time-dependent enhancement of THz emission from exchange-coupled NiO/Fe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144404] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jia Xu, Xianguo Jiang, Shaohua Zhang, Yaxuan Jin, Lei Hao, Ning Yang, Yizheng Wu, Feng Xu, Wendeng Huang, Hao Meng, Johan Åkerman, Yan Zhou, and Chao Zhou

Antiferromagnetic oxide materials are emerging as key ingredients in spintronic devices and ultrafast terahertz (THz) emitters due to their distinct spin dynamics and vanishing net magnetization. Here, we report an irreversible, time-dependent enhancement of THz emission from exchange-coupled NiO/Fe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144404] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Terahertz enhancement in NiO/Fe/Pt heterostructures driven by laser-induced magneto-ionic interfacial reconstruction

item.title: Homomorphism, substructure, and ideal: Elementary but rigorous aspects of renormalization group or hierarchical structure of topological orders
item.description:
Author(s): Yoshiki Fukusumi and Yuma Furuta

We propose a general quantum Hamiltonian formalism of a renormalization group (RG) flow with an emphasis on generalized symmetry by interpreting the elementary relationship between homomorphism, quotient ring, and projection. In our formalism, the noninvertible nature of the ideal of a fusion ring r…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155103] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026

In our formalism, the noninvertible nature of the ideal of a fusion ring r…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155103] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026

item.title: Charge density wave induced gapped nodal line
item.description:
Author(s): Sergey Alekseev, Lei Chen, and Jennifer Cano

We investigate the interplay between charge density wave (CDW) order and topological nodal-line states in square-net materials. Our Ginzburg-Landau theory predicts a CDW instability that generically opens a gap at the Fermi energy while preserving the nodal-line crossing. However, as the Fermi level…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155106] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sergey Alekseev, Lei Chen, and Jennifer Cano

We investigate the interplay between charge density wave (CDW) order and topological nodal-line states in square-net materials. Our Ginzburg-Landau theory predicts a CDW instability that generically opens a gap at the Fermi energy while preserving the nodal-line crossing. However, as the Fermi level…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155106] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026

item.title: Paramagnetic state of the kagome Kondo lattice compound ${\mathrm{YbV}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{6}$: A $^{51}\mathrm{V}$ nuclear magnetic resonance study
item.description:
Author(s): S. Park, H. Sakai, S. Hosoi, S. M. Thomas, S. Kambe, Y. Tokunaga, A. P. Dioguardi, J. D. Thompson, F. Ronning, M. Kimata, T. Furukawa, T. Sasaki, E. D. Bauer, and M. Hirata

The Kondo lattice compound ${\mathrm{YbV}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{6}$ has recently been discovered to exhibit antiferromagnetic order at ${T}_{\mathrm{N}}≈0.4\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\mathrm{K}$ and features a layered structure that combines a triangular lattice network of ${\mathrm{Yb}}^{3+}$ ions wi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155107] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S. Park, H. Sakai, S. Hosoi, S. M. Thomas, S. Kambe, Y. Tokunaga, A. P. Dioguardi, J. D. Thompson, F. Ronning, M. Kimata, T. Furukawa, T. Sasaki, E. D. Bauer, and M. Hirata

The Kondo lattice compound YbV6Sn6 has recently been discovered to exhibit antiferromagnetic order at TN≈0.4K and features a layered structure that combines a triangular lattice network of Yb3+ ions with a vanadium-based kagome network. We report a V51 nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) study of the p…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155107] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Paramagnetic state of the kagome Kondo lattice compound ${\mathrm{YbV}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{6}$: A $^{51}\mathrm{V}$ nuclear magnetic resonance study
item.dc:creator: S. Park, H. Sakai, S. Hosoi, S. M. Thomas, S. Kambe, Y. Tokunaga, A. P. Dioguardi, J. D. Thompson, F. Ronning, M. Kimata, T. Furukawa, T. Sasaki, E. D. Bauer, and M. Hirata

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Microscopic origin of period-four stripe charge density wave in the kagome metal ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yuma Murata, Rina Tazai, Youichi Yamakawa, Seiichiro Onari, and Hiroshi Kontani

The interplay between unconventional density waves and exotic superconductivity has attracted growing interest. Kagome superconductors $A{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ ($A=\mathrm{K},\mathrm{Rb},\mathrm{Cs}$) offer a platform for studying quantum phase transitions and the resulting symmetry brea…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155108] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuma Murata, Rina Tazai, Youichi Yamakawa, Seiichiro Onari, and Hiroshi Kontani

The interplay between unconventional density waves and exotic superconductivity has attracted growing interest. Kagome superconductors AV3Sb5 (A=K,Rb,Cs) offer a platform for studying quantum phase transitions and the resulting symmetry breaking. Among these quantum phases, the 4a0 stripe charge-den…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155108] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Microscopic origin of period-four stripe charge density wave in the kagome metal ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$
item.dc:creator: Yuma Murata, Rina Tazai, Youichi Yamakawa, Seiichiro Onari, and Hiroshi Kontani
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Flux-driven charge and spin transport in a dimerized Hubbard ring with Fibonacci modulation
item.description:
Author(s): Souvik Roy, Soumya Ranjan Padhi, and Tapan Mishra

We study quantum transport in a one-dimensional Hubbard ring with dimerized nearest-neighbor hoppings and a Fibonacci-modulated on-site potential. For a noninteracting case our analysis reveals that at half filling, the charge current along with the Drude weight decreases with increasing on-site pot…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155401] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Souvik Roy, Soumya Ranjan Padhi, and Tapan Mishra

We study quantum transport in a one-dimensional Hubbard ring with dimerized nearest-neighbor hoppings and a Fibonacci-modulated on-site potential. For a noninteracting case our analysis reveals that at half filling, the charge current along with the Drude weight decreases with increasing on-site pot…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155401] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Flux-driven charge and spin transport in a dimerized Hubbard ring with Fibonacci modulation
item.dc:creator: Souvik Roy, Soumya Ranjan Padhi, and Tapan Mishra
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Elevated Hall responses as indicators of edge reconstruction
item.description:
Author(s): Sampurna Karmakar, Amulya Ratnakar, and Sourin Das

We investigate edge reconstruction scenarios in the $ν=1$ quantum Hall state, focusing on configurations with upstream and downstream charge and neutral modes. Our analysis shows that the coexistence of upstream charge and neutral modes in a multiterminal geometry can cause pronounced deviations fro…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155402] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sampurna Karmakar, Amulya Ratnakar, and Sourin Das

We investigate edge reconstruction scenarios in the ν=1 quantum Hall state, focusing on configurations with upstream and downstream charge and neutral modes. Our analysis shows that the coexistence of upstream charge and neutral modes in a multiterminal geometry can cause pronounced deviations from …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155402] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Elevated Hall responses as indicators of edge reconstruction
item.dc:creator: Sampurna Karmakar, Amulya Ratnakar, and Sourin Das

item.title: Strain-tunable Berry curvature dipole and doping-enhanced shift current in two-dimensional Janus ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{TeSe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Zishan Liao, Zhiming Xu, Erqing Wang, and H. Huang

The recently synthesized two-dimensional Janus material ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{TeSe}}_{2}$ offers great potential for nonlinear quantum transport owing to its intrinsically noncentrosymmetric crystal structure. However, its inherent ${\mathcal{C}}_{3v}$ symmetry enforces a cancellation of the Be…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165103] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zishan Liao, Zhiming Xu, Erqing Wang, and H. Huang

The recently synthesized two-dimensional Janus material Bi2TeSe2 offers great potential for nonlinear quantum transport owing to its intrinsically noncentrosymmetric crystal structure. However, its inherent C3v symmetry enforces a cancellation of the Berry curvature dipole (BCD), the key quantity un…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165103] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Strain-tunable Berry curvature dipole and doping-enhanced shift current in two-dimensional Janus ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{TeSe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Zishan Liao, Zhiming Xu, Erqing Wang, and H. Huang

item.title: Spin-fluctuation-mediated chiral $d+i{d}^{′}$-wave superconductivity in the $α$–${\mathcal{T}}_{3}$ lattice with an incipient flat band
item.description:
Author(s): Masataka Kakoi and Kazuhiko Kuroki

We study anisotropic superconductivity in the nearly quarter-filled $α$–${\mathcal{T}}_{3}$ lattice. We analyze an extended Hubbard model with off-site attractive interactions within the mean-field framework and find two distinct chiral $d+i{d}^{′}$-wave superconducting phases characterized by diffe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165104] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Masataka Kakoi and Kazuhiko Kuroki

We study anisotropic superconductivity in the nearly quarter-filled α–T3 lattice. We analyze an extended Hubbard model with off-site attractive interactions within the mean-field framework and find two distinct chiral d+id′-wave superconducting phases characterized by different Chern numbers. We fur…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165104] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-fluctuation-mediated chiral $d+i{d}^{′}$-wave superconductivity in the $α$–${\mathcal{T}}_{3}$ lattice with an incipient flat band
item.dc:creator: Masataka Kakoi and Kazuhiko Kuroki
